markdown:
1. The **demand** **curve** is the **graphical representation** of the **negative** **relationship** between the **price** of a good or service the **quantity** of it that is **demanded**.
2. This relationship is described by the **law of demand **(2.1.1).
3. Since higher prices lead to less quantity demanded (negative relationship), the  **demand curve **is downward-slopping (Figure 1).

![Figure 1: Demand curve ($D$)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/12251ae7212f8900d7ac22655ccfdf58f31c5e5d-2454x2205.png)

The figure above showcases a demand curve that is **downward-sloping** due to the **law of demand**:

1. For a price of $2$, consumers are **willing and able** to buy $50$ units of the good (it is cheap and has a lot of utility!).
2. However, as the price **rises** to $3,4$ and $5$, the quantity demanded by consumers **decreases** to $40, 30$ and $20$ respectively. This is because of (2.1.1): 
  1. The **law of diminishing marginal utility.**
  2.  The **income **and **substitution effect. **

<callout type="tip">#### Drawing the demand curve


Three **important** points to ensure when drawing the a **demand** **curve** diagram:

1. Make sure to **label the axis** (holds for any diagram). In this case, for any diagram representing the market for a good or service:
  1. Prices $P$ will be on the $y$-axis (add the currency being used too).
  2. Quantity $Q$ will be on the $x$-axis
2. The **demand curve** should show an **downward-sloping **curve (negative relationship) such that a decrease in price leads to an increase in the quantity demanded.
3. If you're showcasing a specific point on the curve, make sure to **label the price and quantity** associated with it clearly. For example label the price $P_1$ and quantity as $Q_{s1}$</callout>

<callout type="note">1. It is **important **to note that the demand curve **_doesn't _**indicate anything about the **actual** quantity consumers are consuming: 
2. The curve indicates what quantities consumers are** willing and able** to **potentially** buy in the market.</callout>
